item.text:
I couldn’t apply the change because this session’s filesystem is read-only and approvals are disabled, so writes are blocked.

The intended patch is:

```python
# demo/rate_limiter.py
    def reset(self) -> None:
        self.calls.clear()
```

And the test to add:

```python
def test_reset_clears_calls_and_allows_again(self):
    limiter = RateLimiter(limit=2, window=10)

    with patch("demo.rate_limiter.time.time", side_effect=[0, 1, 2, 3]):
        self.assertTrue(limiter.allow())
        self.assertTrue(limiter.allow())
        self.assertFalse(limiter.allow())

        limiter.reset()

        self.assertEqual(limiter.calls, [])
        self.assertTrue(limiter.allow())

    self.assertEqual(limiter.calls, [3])
```

This keeps `allow()` unchanged and verifies `reset()` clears stored calls so the limiter can accept a new call immediately.
